workflow.name: brownfield-onboarding

critical.mandate: Document existing state before proposing changes
Stories should only cover gaps, not re-implement existing features
All diagrams must use Mermaid syntax — no ASCII art, no text descriptions of diagrams
All API documentation must use Swagger/OpenAPI format
Limit flow diagrams to 3-5 key flows to avoid output bloat
Subagent completion MUST NOT auto-advance the workflow. After all subagents return, pause for user review before proceeding.

step.title: Deep Project Discovery
step.action: Invoke document-project workflow to scan codebase
Capture: tech stack, architecture, existing features, code quality, conventions
API Detection: scan for route definitions, controllers, OpenAPI/Swagger specs. Set {has_apis} flag (true/false)
Event Detection: scan for Kafka, RabbitMQ, SNS-SQS, Redis pub-sub, NATS, or other messaging patterns. Set {has_events} flag (true/false)
Dependency Detection: scan for outbound HTTP clients, SDKs, service URLs, database connections. Set {has_external_deps} flag (true/false)
Frontend Detection: scan for React, Angular, Vue, Flutter, SwiftUI, UI frameworks, CSS/styling. Set {has_frontend} flag (true/false)
Testing infrastructure: identify test framework, coverage config, test count, test patterns
CI/CD: identify GitHub Actions, Jenkins, Docker, Terraform, or other pipeline files

Generate brownfield assessment: read {installed_path}/../../templates/brownfield-assessment-template.md — capture component inventory, technical debt, migration constraints, coexistence strategy, and adoption path. Output to {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-assessment.md
step.template-output: Generate enhanced project documentation following the project documentation conventions. Include all standard sections plus: detected capability flags (has_apis, has_events, has_external_deps, has_frontend), testing infrastructure summary, and CI/CD pipeline summary.

step.title: Parallel Documentation Subagents
step.action: Spawn subagents in parallel using the Agent tool with multiple calls in a single message. Only spawn subagents for detected capabilities.

If {has_apis}: spawn subagent — "Scan the codebase at {project-path} for API routes, controllers, and OpenAPI/Swagger specs. Read {installed_path}/../../templates/api-documentation-template.md for the output format. Check if an OpenAPI spec file already exists — if so, validate it against actual routes; if not, generate an OpenAPI 3.x spec from code. Document all endpoints (method, path, handler, auth, parameters, request/response schemas, error formats). Include a Mermaid API flow diagram. List undocumented endpoints as gaps. All API docs must use Swagger/OpenAPI format. Output to {planning_artifacts}/api-documentation.md"

If {has_frontend}: spawn subagent — "Scan the codebase at {project-path} for UI frameworks, components, design patterns, and styling. Read {installed_path}/../../templates/ux-design-assessment-template.md for the output format. Document existing UI patterns, navigation structure (Mermaid sitemap), interaction patterns, accessibility (WCAG compliance, ARIA, keyboard nav). Identify UX gaps — propose improvements for gaps only, not redesign of working patterns. Output to {planning_artifacts}/ux-design.md"

If {has_events}: spawn subagent — "Scan the codebase at {project-path} for messaging patterns (Kafka, RabbitMQ, SNS-SQS, Redis pub-sub, NATS). Read {installed_path}/../../templates/event-catalog-template.md for the output format. Document messaging infrastructure, produced/consumed events with schemas, external events, delivery guarantees (retry, DLQ, idempotency). Include Mermaid event flow diagrams for 2-3 key flows. Output to {planning_artifacts}/event-catalog.md"

Spawn subagent (always) — "Scan the codebase at {project-path} for external dependencies. Read {installed_path}/../../templates/dependency-map-template.md for the output format. Document external service dependencies (APIs consumed, auth, health checks), infrastructure dependencies (databases, caches, queues), key library dependencies (ORM, auth lib — check version currency and CVE risk). Create Mermaid dependency graph. Document contracts, SLAs, and fallback strategies. Identify dependency risks. Output to {planning_artifacts}/dependency-map.md. Then load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-dependency-audit.xml, read its entire contents, and follow the flow steps EXACTLY to generate a dependency audit report."

Wait for all subagents to complete. Verify each expected output file exists: dependency-map.md at {planning_artifacts}/ (always), api-documentation.md at {planning_artifacts}/ (if has_apis), ux-design.md at {planning_artifacts}/ (if has_frontend), event-catalog.md at {planning_artifacts}/ (if has_events), dependency-audit-{date}.md at {test_artifacts}/ (always).

If any subagent failed to write its output file: the orchestrator MUST write the file on the subagent's behalf using the paths declared in workflow.yaml output.artifacts. Do NOT use hardcoded paths — read the workflow config to determine the correct output directory for each artifact. Specifically: dependency-audit goes to {test_artifacts}/, all other Step 2 artifacts go to {planning_artifacts}/.
step.template-output: After all subagents complete, summarize: which subagents ran, artifacts produced, file paths, and any errors. This checkpoint ensures user reviews subagent results before NFR assessment.

step.title: NFR Assessment & Performance Test Plan
step.action: Spawn a subagent — "Analyze the codebase at {project-path} for non-functional requirements. Read {installed_path}/../../templates/nfr-assessment-template.md for the output format. Assess: code quality (linting, complexity, duplication), security posture (dependency vulnerabilities, secrets handling, auth quality — load security-basics skill section vulnerability-scanning if needed), performance (bundle size if frontend, query patterns, caching, resource management), accessibility (ARIA, semantic HTML, keyboard nav if frontend), test coverage (framework, count, coverage %, untested areas, quality), CI/CD (build pipeline, deploy strategy, environments, IaC). Create NFR Baseline Summary Table with measured values (not placeholders). Output to {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md. Then create a performance test plan: load {project-root}/_gaia/testing/knowledge/performance/k6-patterns.md for load testing patterns. If {has_frontend}: also load {project-root}/_gaia/testing/knowledge/performance/lighthouse-ci.md. Define performance budgets (P50/P95/P99) based on NFR baselines, load test scenarios (gradual, spike, soak), backend profiling targets (slow queries, N+1, connection pools), CI performance gates. If {has_frontend}: define Core Web Vitals targets (LCP under 2.5s, INP under 200ms, CLS under 0.1). Output to {test_artifacts}/performance-test-plan-{date}.md"

When subagent returns: verify both {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md and {test_artifacts}/performance-test-plan-{date}.md exist. If the subagent failed to write files, the orchestrator MUST write them to {test_artifacts}/ as declared in workflow.yaml output.artifacts — do NOT fall back to {planning_artifacts}/.

step.title: Create PRD for Gaps
step.action: Read the PRD template from {installed_path}/../../templates/prd-template.md
Read upstream artifacts to inform gap analysis:
— nfr-assessment.md → NFR section gets real "Current Baseline" and "Target" columns
— api-documentation.md (if exists) → extract API gaps (undocumented endpoints, missing validation)
— event-catalog.md (if exists) → extract messaging gaps (missing DLQ, missing schemas)
— dependency-map.md → extract dependency risks (outdated versions, missing health checks)
— dependency-audit-{date}.md → extract critical/high findings as gap requirements (CVEs, outdated packages, license conflicts)
— ux-design.md (if exists) → extract UX gaps (accessibility violations, inconsistent patterns)
Invoke create-prd logic in brownfield mode: fill every section with gap-focused content only
Overview = existing project summary + what gaps this PRD addresses
Goals = gap closure goals only. Non-Goals = existing features that will NOT be re-implemented
User Stories = stories for gap features only, not existing functionality
Functional Requirements = gap requirements organized by priority
Non-Functional Requirements = NFR gaps with current baseline and target from {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md

If {planning_artifacts}/prd.md already exists: WARN user — "A PRD already exists. Continuing will overwrite it with brownfield gap content. Choose: (a) overwrite, (b) save as prd-brownfield-gaps.md instead." If user chooses (b), adjust template-output path accordingly.
step.template-output: Generate a PRD following the standard template structure. YAML frontmatter MUST include: mode: brownfield, baseline_version: {version from package.json or inferred}, focus: gap-filling. Add "Mode: Brownfield — gaps only" to the header. All sections must follow the template format but contain only gap-focused content. NFR section must include "Current Baseline" and "Target" columns with real values from {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md. Include a Priority Matrix section mapping each gap to priority/effort/impact.

step.title: Adversarial Review & PRD Refinement
step.action: Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/tasks/review-adversarial.xml. Read its entire contents. Target: {planning_artifacts}/prd.md. Target label: prd. Follow the task instructions EXACTLY." This is required — findings will be incorporated back into the PRD.
When subagent returns: verify adversarial-review-prd-{date}.md exists in {planning_artifacts}/
Read {planning_artifacts}/adversarial-review-prd-{date}.md — extract critical and high severity findings
For each critical/high finding: add as a new requirement or refine an existing requirement in the PRD
Add a "## Review Findings Incorporated" section to the PRD listing each finding, its severity, and how it was addressed
step.template-output: Update the PRD with adversarial review findings incorporated. Critical/high findings become new requirements or refine existing ones. Add "Review Findings Incorporated" section.

step.title: Architecture
step.action: Architecture is created using the Phase 3 workflow which auto-detects brownfield mode from the PRD "Mode: Brownfield" header set in step 4.

If {planning_artifacts}/architecture.md already exists: WARN user — "An architecture document already exists. Continuing will overwrite it with the brownfield version. Choose: (a) overwrite, (b) save as architecture-brownfield.md instead." If user chooses (b), instruct the subagent to output to {planning_artifacts}/architecture-brownfield.md.

Spawn a subagent using the Agent tool: "Load {project-root}/_gaia/core/engine/workflow.xml, then process {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/3-solutioning/create-architecture/workflow.yaml as workflow-config. Run in YOLO mode. Follow the workflow engine instructions EXACTLY." When subagent returns: verify the architecture file exists at the chosen path.

After architecture is generated: verify it has YAML frontmatter with mode: brownfield, baseline_version: {version}, and update_scope: [list of components being modified]. If these fields are missing, append them to the YAML frontmatter block.

The remaining Phase 3 sequence must be run after this workflow completes: /gaia-review-api (optional, if APIs) → /gaia-adversarial → /gaia-test-design → /gaia-test-framework (optional) → /gaia-create-epics → /gaia-threat-model → /gaia-infra-design → /gaia-trace → /gaia-ci-setup → /gaia-readiness-check

step.title: Bootstrap Val Ground Truth
step.action: Check if Val is installed: verify {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/agents/validator.md exists AND {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ directory exists

check.if: validator.md not found OR validator-sidecar/ not found

step.check: Skip Step 7 silently — Val is not installed. Brownfield onboarding continues without ground truth bootstrap.
step.ask: Step 7: Bootstrap Val ground truth from brownfield assessment? [y/n]
step.action: Check if /gaia-refresh-ground-truth workflow exists: look for {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/workflows/*/refresh-ground-truth/ directory
step.check: Val ground truth bootstrap skipped — refresh-ground-truth workflow not available. Run /gaia-refresh-ground-truth manually after E8-S9 is implemented. Brownfield onboarding completes successfully.
step.action: Invoke /gaia-refresh-ground-truth to scan the filesystem and populate framework inventory facts (workflows, agents, skills, commands) into ground-truth.md
Load brownfield-extraction section of ground-truth-management skill JIT from {project-root}/_gaia/lifecycle/skills/ground-truth-management.md

Read available brownfield artifacts and extract project-specific facts. For each artifact, check if the file exists before reading — skip missing artifacts without error: — {planning_artifacts}/brownfield-assessment.md: extract tech stack, dependencies, file counts, project structure → file-inventory and variable-inventory entries — {planning_artifacts}/project-documentation.md: extract architecture patterns, conventions, config values → structural-pattern and cross-reference entries — {test_artifacts}/nfr-assessment.md (if present): extract performance targets, security requirements → variable-inventory entries

Write extracted project facts to {memory_path}/validator-sidecar/ground-truth.md. If ground-truth.md already exists with content: merge new facts with existing entries — add new facts, update changed facts, flag removed facts — never destructive overwrite. Follow merge semantics from ground-truth-management conflict-resolution section. If ground-truth.md is empty or new: write all extracted facts as initial seed entries with verification count = 1.
Report: "Seeded {N} ground-truth entries from brownfield artifacts + filesystem scan"

workflow.next-step:
Run the remaining Phase 3 sequence after this workflow: 1. /gaia-review-api — review API design against standards (optional, if APIs exist) 2. /gaia-adversarial — adversarial review of architecture 3. /gaia-test-design — create risk-based test plan (required before epics) 4. /gaia-test-framework — setup test framework (optional) 5. /gaia-create-epics — create epics and stories for gap requirements 6. /gaia-threat-model — security threat model 7. /gaia-infra-design — infrastructure design 8. /gaia-trace — traceability matrix 9. /gaia-ci-setup — CI pipeline setup 10. /gaia-readiness-check — implementation readiness validation
WARNING: If Phase 3 artifacts already exist from a prior greenfield setup (test-plan.md, epics-and-stories.md), running these workflows will overwrite them. Consider backing up existing artifacts or using the brownfield PRD path to generate separate brownfield-specific outputs.
